title: agropyron wangii
intro: Agropyron wangii, also known as Elymus sibiricus, is a type of perennial grass native to the steppes of Siberia and Mongolia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is valued for its ability to aid in respiratory health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, with the roots and leaves being used separately to create various herbal remedies.
